Title: Graphene enhances artemisinin production in the traditional medicinal plant Artemisia annua via dynamic physiological processes and miRNA regulation

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: glandular secreting trichome initiation, thereby indirectly controlling artemisinin biosynthesis and storage capacity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positive regulator of GST initiation; higher expression correlates with increased GST density and enhanced artemisinin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: positive regulator of GST initiation; decisive because GSTs are the biosynthetic and storage structures for artemisinin
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of GST initiation program
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: induces downstream core GST-initiation gene AaHD1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: induced by graphene treatment in leaves; increased in graphene-treated AaMYB17-RNAi lines relative to untreated RNAi lines
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR under graphene treatment; 5′ RACE validating miR828-directed cleavage; dual-luciferase assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; AaMYB17-RNAi lines with and without graphene treatment; GST density phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase GST density and artemisinin yield; can be activated directly or protected from miR828/siRNA suppression; suitable for nanomaterial-assisted or non-transgenic engineering of trichome-specific production traits
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: graphene at 10 or 20 mg/L; exogenous 1 mM H2O2; H2O2 plus 100 μM GSH
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of leaves; raw sequencing data in GSA: CRA010136
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter used for AaMYB17-LUC reporter in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaMYB17 expression was significantly induced by graphene treatment. 2. miR828 directly cleaved AaMYB17, as shown by 5′ RACE and dual-luciferase assays. 3. Relief of miR828 repression increased GST initiation, which supported higher artemisinin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AaMYB17 was also previously characterized by RNAi; graphene partially counteracted RNAi-associated suppression, consistent with inhibition of Dicer-dependent small-RNA biogenesis.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: post-transcriptional repression of GST initiation via target cleavage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates GST density and thereby limits artemisinin accumulation potential
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: strong repressor of GST initiation through cleavage of AaMYB17
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: post-transcriptional gene silencing via cleavage of target mRNA
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: graphene-induced H2O2/ROS; Dicer downregulation; exogenous H2O2; GSH partially rescues H2O2 effect
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mature miR828 is reduced by graphene and by exogenous H2O2 in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mature miRNA qRT-PCR; 5′ RACE cleavage mapping on AaMYB17; dual-luciferase assay; transgenic overexpression of miR828 precursor followed by GST density analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Repressing miR828 or editing its target site in AaMYB17 could increase GST density and artemisinin yield; caution that broad miRNA suppression may affect other traits
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: graphene at 10 or 20 mg/L; 1 mM H2O2; 1 mM H2O2 plus 100 μM GSH
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq indicated Dicer downregulation; raw sequencing data in GSA: CRA010136
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: miR828 overexpression reduced GST density by about 60%; broad suppression or alteration of miRNA pathways may have pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functions through the Dicer-dependent small RNA biogenesis and RNA-induced silencing pathway
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: miR828 precursor overexpression construct pHB-miR828; specific promoter not reported
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Graphene and H2O2 reduced mature miR828 abundance. 2. miR828 directly cleaves AaMYB17 mRNA. 3. Overexpression of miR828 markedly reduced GST density, linking this miRNA to lower artemisinin production capacity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study positions miR828 as the focal small-RNA regulator within a broader graphene/ROS-dependent suppression of miRNA biogenesis.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: miRNA biogenesis affecting GST initiation regulators
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation reduces miRNA maturation, relieving repression of GST-promoting genes and increasing artemisinin production capacity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent controller linking graphene-induced ROS to the miR828–AaMYB17 module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes maturation of miR828 and other miRNAs that repress GST initiation genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Dicer gene expression is reduced in graphene-treated plants; AaDCL1 is downregulated after 1 mM H2O2 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq heatmap of Dicers; qRT-PCR of AaDCL1 after H2O2 treatment; downstream mature miRNA measurements
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A tunable leverage point to modulate small-RNA repression of GST traits, but broad DCL suppression may cause extensive pleiotropy and should be applied cautiously
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: graphene at 10 or 20 mg/L; exogenous 1 mM H2O2
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad inhibition of Dicer likely affects many miRNAs/siRNAs beyond miR828, creating pleiotropic and off-target developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functions within the small RNA biogenesis machinery
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Graphene reduced expression of Dicer genes involved in miRNA biogenesis. 2. H2O2 treatment also downregulated AaDCL1. 3. Reduced DCL activity likely lowers mature miR828 accumulation and thereby derepresses AaMYB17 to increase GST density.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats Dicers mechanistically as upstream mediators of graphene/ROS effects rather than as direct trait-engineering targets.
